title: Classify calls with thinking enabled return empty text — model puts verdict in thinking block
body: classifyPrompt() passes thinkingEnabled: true to complete(). With thinking enabled, the model can place its entire response (including the CLEAN/FLAG/NEW verdict) inside the thinking block and return no text content. extractText() filters for type === 'text' only, so thinking content is invisible. parseVerdict('') hits the unrecognized format path and returns verdict: 'error'. Observed on fragility monitor: 'classify failed: Unrecognized verdict format: ""'. Immediate fix: extractText() should fall back to thinking block content when no text blocks are present. This issue is also a symptom of issue-025 — unifying the classify path with agent specs eliminates this bug class entirely since the agent output pipeline already handles thinking extraction.
resolved_by: extractResponseText() replaces extractText() in classifyPrompt() — falls back to thinking block content when no text parts present. Agent path uses extractResponseText() natively. Both paths now handle thinking-only responses.
